publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation by the library in the upper right corner.

list.item:
9
3, stub after 5 6, stub after 2 The structure of the quire is not entirely clear, as two further stubs are visible between .

binding.decoNote: In the original boards, covered with blind-tooled leather.

history.provenance: The manuscript had very probably belonged to a church in the Gondar area, from where it was taken by Tewodros and brought to Madḫāne ʿĀlam. It was then looted by the British Napier expedition in 1868.

bibl.title: Catalogue of the Ethiopic Manuscripts in the British Museum Acquired since the Year 1847

msItem.title: Rāʾǝya [ʾǝgzǝʾtǝna] Māryām
msItem.note: The catalogue description does not allow for the correct identification of this manuscript’s codicological units. It is possibly a composite manuscript, but treated here in a simple manuscript description until examination to avoid wrong delimitations of units.

msItem.title: Maṣḥafa śǝrʿāt za-waṣʾa ʾǝm-manbara Mārqos ḥawāryā ʾǝm-makāna maʿāllǝqā
Introductory Prayer to the Miracles of Mary
Taʾammǝra Māryām
msItem.note: This manuscript contains 51 miracles before and four after it.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Homily on a potter from Syria
Excerpt from Bǝsrāta Luqās, the dialogue of Mary and the angel

msItem.colophon: The colophon states that this manuscript was written for Zarʾa Yāʿqob, who donated it to a church of Mary. ዘንተ፡ ተአምረ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘአጽሐፈ፡ ንጉሥነ፡ ዘርአ፡ ያዕቆብ፡ ወዘወሀበሂ፡ ከመ፡ ይኩን፡ ለማርያም፡ ወኢያውፅኡ፡ እምኔሃ፡ እስከ፡ ለዓለም፡ ዘእንበለ፡ ለአንብቦ፡ ዘንተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ለእመ፡ አውፅኡ፡ ውጉዛነ፡ ለይኩኑ፡ በአፈ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ እስከ፡ ለዓለም፡ አሜን።

layout.ab: Pricking and ruling are partly visible.
Primary pricks are partly visible, horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.
Pricking and ruling are partly visible.
Primary pricks are partly visible, horizontal pricks are partly visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.
Pricking and ruling are not well visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.
Pricking and ruling are partly visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.desc: Written in a fine bold hand.

The script is described in Äthiopische PaläographieÄthiopistische ForschungenSiegbertUhligStuttgartFranz Steiner Verlag GmbH1988Äthiopistische Forschungen2222CodicologyEthiopicPalaeographybm:Uhlig1988PalaographieCodicologyEthiopicPalaeographybm:Uhlig1988Palaographie344-346.

decoNote.desc: Crude painting of St George of Lydda.

item.desc: On the upper margins, ordinance on the offices of nǝbura ʾǝd and mamhǝr in the monastery of Kǝbrān Gabrǝʾel. William Wright states that it is from the reign of Susǝnyos, however, the text given in Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)Annales d’ÉthiopieMerid Wolde Aregay19851387-102http://www.persee.fr/doc/ethio_0066-2127_1985_num_13_1_923Land tenureSarsa Dengelbm:Merid1985LandChartersLand tenureSarsa Dengelbm:Merid1985LandCharters89 mentions Śarḍa Dǝngǝl.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

item.desc:
Deed of the 16th year of Śarḍa Dǝngǝl, when he was going to war with Yǝsḥaq, giving the land of Gunā to
ʾabbā Bǝʾse ʾƎgziʾabǝḥer and to
ʾabbā Labbāwi Krǝstos.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

item.desc: Renewal by Śarḍa Dǝngǝl of the gʷǝlt of Yawandol granted in the days of Zarʾa Yāʿqob and renewed in the days of Galāwdewos.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)

Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

item.desc: Gʷǝlt by Śarḍa Dǝngǝl, granting the horse land of ʾEnzar, Mānṣar, lower Gadab and upper Gadab, the horse land of the bǝhǝt wadad to Salik.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

item.desc: List of furniture on the upper margin.
Gʷǝlt by Śarḍa Dǝngǝl, granting the land of ʾAšankerā, between Bošā and Gumār to Walatta Nabiyāt and her daughter Sabana Māryām.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie
Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

item.desc: Land grant by ʾIyāsu I, giving the land of Qolalā and Ḥǝnāč̣očč to the church of Dabra ʿƎnqʷ during the tenures of Sinodā and Yoḥannǝs [III].
item.q: ለኪዳነ፡ ምሕረት፡ እንተ፡ ሰመይናሃ፡ ደብረ፡ እንቍ፡

item.desc:
Land grant by Śarḍa Dǝngǝl at the time when ʾAbyātār was mamhǝr
of Dabra Māryām, giving the land of Dǝbri to
ʾabbā Ḥarā

item.desc: The note states that the manuscript belongs to the church of Madḫāne ʿĀlam.

bibl.title: Some inedited land charters of Emperor Särsä Dengel (r. 1563-1597)
Annales d’Éthiopie

msItem.title: ʾƎsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki
msItem.incipit: እሰግድ፡ ለኪ፡ እሰግድ፡ ለኪ፡ እሰግድ፡ ለኪ፡ ኦእግዝእትየ፡ ማርያም፡ በከመ፡ ወደሰኪ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ አፈ፡ ወርቅ፡ በድርሳኑ፡ እንዘ፡ ይብል፡

layout.ab: Primary pricks are visible, horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

item.desc: and were written for Śāhla Rufāʾel.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
